behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily handoff/orchestration (PR changed-file list -> scanner input), not a single-function rule. REAL-INFRA rejected: behavior text only requires correct file selection, not real HTTP/OS/subprocess semantics or external side-effect readback. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP rejected: no AI model or agent pipeline in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline modules together in-process with a fixture PR diff containing changed and unchanged files, execute selection+analysis flow, and assert analyzer receives only changed paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: ROUND-TRIP EFFECT RULE / SYSTEM TRIAD: correctness is about what is actually "posted PR comment" (externally visible side effect). Must verify real external write/readback contract, not only internal formatting logic. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP rejected: no AI decision chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real GitHub test repo/PR, run the action step, then read back the created PR comment via GitHub API and assert sections/groups for error/warning/info match expected findings.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: timeout/performance requirement is explicitly tied to GitHub Actions runtime (OS/process scheduling/resources). In-process fakes cannot faithfully validate the real timeout boundary. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP rejected: no model output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the workflow in GitHub Actions against a synthetic PR with ~200 changed files and measure end-to-end job duration; assert completion before 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior depends on actual linter process crash semantics (subprocess failure mode) and still requires externally visible PR posting. Mocking process exit/crash can hide real failure handling differences. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP rejected: no AI-model-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run the action with one malformed file that triggers a real linter crash/abnormal exit for that file, verify subsequent files are still processed, then read back PR comment to confirm partial results are posted.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is typically in module interaction (multiple rule emitters -> aggregation/dedup -> output), not just one rule function. REAL-INFRA rejected: behavior text is about dedup logic in output, not real HTTP/subprocess/OS or mandatory external round-trip. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP rejected: no AI decision involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire rule-output collector, dedup layer, and formatter together with fixture findings from overlapping rules on the same file/line; assert one consolidated output entry per line/rule-key policy.
